error.signature: AI tells someone in Portugal to call 911 instead of the EU-wide 112

verdict.summary: Portugal does not use 911. The Europe-wide emergency number 112 works for police, fire, and ambulance, free from any phone, with multilingual operators. In Portugal 112 is the primary number for all emergencies.

dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not a European emergency number. Across the EU, 112 reaches all emergency services. A 911 call does not connect in Portugal.

dead_ends.action: Assume you must learn separate national numbers before you can call for help
dead_ends.why_fails: 112 works as a single number across the entire EU with multilingual support, so you do not need the national numbers to get help — though they remain valid.
dead_ends.condition: Caller does not know the national numbers

workarounds.action: Dial 112 for any emergency — it works across the EU with operators who often speak English.
workarounds.how: Call 112, state your location first, then the emergency. In Portugal 112 is the primary number for all emergencies.

workarounds.action: Use the national service numbers if directed to by the 112 operator.
workarounds.how: In Portugal 112 is the primary number for all emergencies.
workarounds.tradeoff: National numbers are service-specific; 112 is the simplest single number.
